qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL API allows unbounded query depth and field count causing denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns GraphQL query complexity validation defaults, not authentication requests with arbitrary unconfigured provider names causing a pre-rejection user-database scan.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unvalidated $regex in find queries enables ReDoS via MongoDB catastrophic backtracking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns REST find-query $regex input reaching MongoDB's regex engine, not unconfigured auth-provider validation occurring after an unindexed user lookup.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is specifically about Parse Server authentication requests using arbitrary unconfigured provider names, where provider validation occurs only after a database query that can full-scan the user collection. The manifest-recognized qualifying findings cover GraphQL query complexity and REST $regex ReDoS instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-g4cf-xj29-wqqr describes unauthenticated authentication requests with arbitrary unconfigured provider names causing database queries before rejection, with the patch moving provider-configuration validation before any query.
graphql-dos-unbounded-queries/vulnerability_report.json describes createComplexityValidationPlugin returning early when graphQLDepth and graphQLFields are -1, allowing expensive GraphQL queries.
redos-via-unvalidated-regex/vulnerability_report.json describes attacker-controlled $regex values flowing from REST find requests into MongoDB PCRE evaluation.
